title: Phase 3: Command injection pattern analysis
description:
Search src/ for exec(), spawn(), child_process usage. Classify each call site as safe (execFile+array args) or risky (shell string interpolation). Document in AUDIT_FINDINGS.md.

Notes: Phase 3 section 3.1 completed by Amp — 30+ imports, ~20 call sites, all use execFile/array args. LOW risk.

title: Phase 3: Path traversal pattern analysis
description:
Search for path.join, fs.readFile, fs.writeFile with user-controlled input. Check for realpath validation, symlink blocking, boundary checks. Document findings.

Notes: Phase 3 section 3.2 completed by Amp — 1841+ path.join calls, strong protections. 2 potential risks in session ID paths. MEDIUM risk.

title: Phase 3: SQL injection pattern analysis
description:
Check SQLite memory system for parameterized queries vs string interpolation. Review FTS input sanitization.

Notes: Phase 3 section 3.3 completed by Amp — all queries parameterized, FTS input sanitized. LOW risk.

title: Phase 3: Weak cryptography analysis
description:
Search for Math.random(), md5, sha1 usage in security contexts. Verify crypto.randomBytes/randomUUID used for tokens/keys.

Notes: Phase 3 section 3.4 completed by Amp — Math.random only cosmetic, sha1 only fingerprinting. LOW risk.

title: Phase 3: Data exposure analysis
description:
Search for console.log/logger calls that might expose tokens, passwords, keys, secrets. Check error messages for credential leaks.

Notes: Phase 3 section 3.5 completed by Amp — no actual secrets logged. LOW risk.

title: Phase 3: Insecure deserialization analysis
description:
Review JSON.parse and YAML.parse call sites. Check for schema validation on parsed inputs, especially from WebSocket/SSE/CLI sources.

Notes: Phase 3 section 3.6 completed by Amp — 296+ JSON.parse calls, 3 medium-risk sites (WebSocket, SSE, CLI params). MEDIUM risk.

title: Phase 4: Review SECURITY.md
description:
Read openclaw/SECURITY.md. Document what security practices are claimed, what scope exclusions exist, and whether a responsible disclosure process is defined.

Notes: Completed. SECURITY.md presents partial/misleading posture — covers Docker/Node.js hardening but omits skill execution model entirely. Basic disclosure process (single Gmail). No explicit threat model.

title: Phase 4: Review security documentation
description:
Read docs/gateway/security and any other security-related docs. Check for secure defaults, hardening guidance, known vulnerability tracking.

Notes: Completed. 21 docs reviewed. Excellent hardening guidance overall. 6 doc-vs-code gaps found — most critical: skill trust boundary undocumented, sandbox off by default and omitted from secure baseline config.

title: Phase 4: Review configuration examples
description:
Check .env.example, config examples in docs/. Ensure no real secrets in examples, secure defaults documented.

Notes: Completed. 16 files reviewed. 11 findings: real phone number in .env.example, weak example gateway tokens, inline API keys normalized, legacy Claude web scraping vars in docker-compose.yml. Good secure defaults overall.

title: Phase 5: Run pnpm audit
description:
Run 'pnpm audit' in openclaw/ directory. Document known CVEs in dependencies. Classify by severity.

Notes: 1,209 deps scanned. 1 moderate CVE (request pkg SSRF, in optional Matrix extension). No critical/high.

title: Phase 5: Dependency count and pinning analysis
description:
Run 'pnpm list --depth=0'. Count direct and transitive deps. Check if versions are pinned (exact) or ranged (^ or ~). Check pnpm-lock.yaml.

Notes: 53 prod + 20 dev deps. 78% caret ranges. 4 pre-release prod deps. 8 native build scripts. typebox version conflict. No pnpm audit in CI.

title: Phase 5: Review vendor/ directory
description:
Inspect openclaw/vendor/ for vendored dependencies. Check what's there, why, and whether they're up to date or modified.

Notes: 1 vendored pkg (Google A2UI v0.8.1). 5 local patches. 2 MEDIUM findings (unsafeHTML, iframe.srcdoc). Unused eval dirs with API key references.

title: Phase 5: Review patches/ directory
description:
Inspect openclaw/patches/ for patched dependencies. Understand what was patched and why. Check for security-relevant patches.

Notes: Empty — only .gitkeep. No pnpm.patchedDependencies. No concerns.

title: Phase 5: Check outdated packages
description:
Run 'pnpm outdated'. Identify packages with known security fixes in newer versions.

Notes: Deferred as standalone task. Key info captured in cricht-011 (security overrides for tar/tough-cookie/qs/fast-xml-parser are maintained). Sole audit CVE has no patch.

title: Phase 6: Run test suite
description:
Run 'pnpm test' in openclaw/. Document pass/fail results. Note any security-related test failures.

Notes: 6,199 passed, 28 failed, 1 skipped. All failures from missing LanceDB native dep (not security). Gateway: 219/219 passed.

title: Phase 6: Run test coverage
description: Run 'pnpm test:coverage'. Document coverage percentages. Identify security-critical code with low coverage.

title: Phase 6: Audit security test quality
description:
Review src/security/ test files (172 tests). Check for negative test cases, injection attack tests, fuzzing, property-based testing. Gap analysis.

Notes: 37 security test files. Strong: SSRF, skill scanner, config audit, webhook verification, Nostr fuzzing. Gaps: no timing-safe verification tests, no credential encryption tests, no runtime sandbox tests, fuzz testing only in extensions.

title: Update review_checklist.json Phase 3
description:
Update review_checklist.json to mark Phase 3 vulnerability pattern analysis as completed. Populate all findings from AUDIT_FINDINGS.md Phase 3 sections.

Notes: Phase 3 work is done in findings but checklist still says not_started

title: Update review_checklist.json Phases 4-6
description: Update review_checklist.json as Phases 4-6 are completed. Keep checklist in sync with actual progress.

title: Spec: Choose language/runtime
description:
Decide language/runtime for secure background agent.

Notes: SBCL (Common Lisp). Live-coding in running daemon via SLIME/SLY. Image-based dev. Condition system for restartable errors. CFFI for wasmtime/OS keychain integration.

title: Spec: Choose initial LLM providers
description:
Decide which LLM providers to support initially.

Notes: Anthropic + OpenAI. Generic CLOS provider protocol so Ollama/local models slot in later.

title: Spec: Choose initial messaging platforms
description:
Decide which messaging platforms to support initially.

Notes: Discord (v0.1), Mastodon (v0.2). No Slack. Generic CLOS channel adapter protocol.

title: Spec: Architecture decision (monolith vs microservice)
description:
Decide monolith or microservice architecture.

Notes: Local monolith (SBCL daemon) + sandboxed WASM skill-runner process(es). Trust boundary at RPC interface.

title: Spec: Define MVP feature set
description:
Define minimum viable product feature set.

Notes: v0.1 'Secure Core': daemon+CLI, WASM skill sandbox, encrypted credentials (keychain+age), encrypted sessions, network egress allowlist, Ed25519 code signing, secure logging. No marketplace, no remote access, no web UI.

title: Spec: Design skill isolation mechanism
description:
Choose isolation approach for skills.

Notes: WASM (WASI) via wasmtime C API called from separate runner process. Host functions: log, kv, http-allowlist, get-secret. Capability manifest in TOML per skill bundle.

title: Spec: Design credential encryption
description:
Choose credential storage approach.

Notes: OS keychain (libsecret/Security.framework via CFFI) primary. age-encrypted file fallback for headless. Daemon mediates all credential access. Skills never see credentials directly.

title: Spec: Design network egress policy
description:
Design network policy for skills.

Notes: WASM sandbox has no network. All HTTP via daemon-owned host function with domain+method+path allowlist per skill. Every request logged with skill identity.

title: Spec: Design code signing for skills
description:
Design code signing infrastructure.

Notes: Ed25519 via ironclad. Trust store at ~/.agent/trusted-keys/. Only signed skills execute. One project root key initially. Rotation documented early.

title: Spec: Design session encryption
description:
Design encrypted session storage.

Notes: age-encrypted session store. Configurable retention (7/30/90 days or no-store). Sessions inaccessible to skills. Identity key at ~/.agent/identity.key (0600).

title: Spec: Design command execution without shell
description:
Design direct exec with argument arrays only.

Notes: sb-ext:run-program with argument lists. No shell interpretation. Pipes/redirects handled by daemon if needed, never by skills.

title: Spec: Design secure logging
description:
Design log system with private directory.

Notes: ~/.agent/logs/ with 0700 dir, 0600 files. Structured JSON lines via log4cl. Redaction built-in. Append-only mode. Future: remote shipping.

title: Spec: Design marketplace policy
description:
Design marketplace controls.

Notes: No marketplace in v0.1. Ed25519 signing mandatory. No auto-install. No remote skill installation without explicit user action.

title: Spec: Design environment variable isolation
description:
Design scoped secret injection.

Notes: WASM sandbox has no env access. Secrets injected via get-secret host function with manifest declaration + daemon approval. Config loaded only from ~/.agent/.

title: Write final audit report
description:
Synthesize all findings into a clean final report. Executive summary, methodology, findings by severity, recommendations, appendices. Suitable for Camosun College submission.

Notes: Closed 2026-02-07.

title: Create remediation plan
description:
Prioritized list of fixes for OpenClaw based on audit findings. Map each finding to a concrete recommendation with effort estimate.

Notes: Closed 2026-02-07.

title: Create student handout
description:
Key lessons from the audit distilled into a teaching handout for Camosun College students. Cover supply chain attacks, sandboxing, credential security.

Notes: Closed 2026-02-07.

title: Name the project
description:
Choose a project name with butler/valet connotations. Must be unambiguously web-searchable.

Notes: Crichton — from 'The Admirable Crichton' by J.M. Barrie. The quietly competent servant. Candidates considered: Wadsworth, Figaro, Crichton, Boswell, Bramwell, Pennyforth.

title: Phase 1: ASDF project scaffold
description:
Set up crichton/ with quickproject. ASDF system def, package structure, module layout matching Phase 1 spec (config, logging, daemon, cli).

Notes: quickproject scaffold + 8 source files. All deps pulled via Quicklisp (cffi, bordeaux-threads, usocket, ironclad, shasht, log4cl, dexador, hunchentoot). System compiles clean. CLI help/version/doctor/status working.

title: Phase 1: Config loading (TOML)
description:
Implement TOML config parser for ~/.crichton/config.toml. Only load from *agent-home*, never CWD. Stub exists in config/loader.lisp.

Notes: cl-toml added to .asd. TOML->nested-plist conversion with key normalization (underscore->hyphen, keyword). Deep merge with defaults. config-get/config-section-get accessors exported. ~/.crichton/config.toml created.

title: Phase 1: Structured JSON logging
description:
Configure log4cl to write JSON lines to ~/.crichton/logs/ with 0600 file permissions. Implement secret redaction.

Notes: Custom json-lines-appender subclassing log4cl:appender. Writes to daemon.jsonl (0600 perms, 0700 dir). shasht for JSON serialization. cl-ppcre redaction patterns for bearer tokens, sk- keys, api_key/secret/password/token. Idempotent setup (removes previous appender on reload).

title: Phase 1: Swank/Slynk connectivity
description:
Integrate Swank server startup into daemon lifecycle. Auto-start on daemon start if configured.

Notes: Swank port read from config (config-section-get :daemon :swank-port, default 4005). start-swank made idempotent via *swank-port* guard. Called from start-daemon. Swank loaded dynamically via asdf:load-system.

title: Phase 1: save-lisp-and-die binary
description:
Build standalone crichton binary via save-lisp-and-die. Test CLI invocation from shell.

Notes: Binary at ~/bin/crichton (symlink). 16MB compressed. CLI commands all working. systemd service at ~/.config/systemd/user/crichton.service, enabled at boot. Rebuilt with cricht-039/040/041 changes.

title: Phase 2: wasmtime CFFI bindings
description:
Thin CFFI bindings to wasmtime C API. Engine, store, module, instance, func_call, error handling. Vendor wasmtime v41.0.2 C API tarball.

Notes: wasm/ffi.lisp: 20+ foreign function defs. wasm/engine.lisp: run-wasm-module high-level wrapper. Tested: WAT → compile → instantiate → call → i32 result = 42. Proper unwind-protect cleanup of engine/store/module.

title: Phase 2: Runner process with unix socket RPC
description:
Separate SBCL runner process (same binary, runner entrypoint). NDJSON over unix socket. Daemon spawns runner, sends skill invocation requests, receives results.

Notes: 3 new modules: rpc/protocol.lisp (NDJSON framing, 8MB size cap, shasht JSON, cl-base64 for binary), runner/server.lisp (unix socket server via sb-bsd-sockets, ping+invoke ops, WAT and WASM bytes support), daemon/runner-client.lisp (spawn via sb-ext:run-program, connect with retries, synchronous invoke API). New: run-wasm-bytes-with-host-fns in wasm/engine.lisp for pre-compiled WASM. Entry point: crichton runner --socket /path/to.sock dispatched from crichton.lisp. All 4 tests pass: ping, WAT invoke (3+4=7), host-log WAT (hello from wasm, returns 42), unknown-op error.

title: Phase 2: Host function implementation
description:
Implement host functions callable from WASM: log, kv_get, kv_set, http_request_allowlisted, get_secret. Use wasmtime linker API to define host funcs.

Notes: Host log function proven end-to-end. ffi.lisp: full union-based struct defs (wasmtime-val 32B, wasmtime-extern 32B), ABI assertions, val/extern accessors, wasmtime_caller_export_get/context, wasmtime_memory_data/data_size bindings. engine.lisp: cffi:defcallback host-log-callback (i32 level + i32 ptr + i32 len), reads string from WASM linear memory via caller_export_get+memory_data, dispatches to log4cl. run-wasm-with-host-fns uses linker API. Tested: WASM calls env.log, Lisp receives 'hello from wasm', returns 42.

title: Phase 2: Capability manifest parsing
description:
Parse skill.toml capability manifests via cl-toml. Validate declared capabilities against policy. Enforce before skill execution.

Notes: skills/manifest.lisp: parse-skill-manifest via cl-toml:parse-file, TOML-to-plist conversion with stringp-before-vectorp fix, manifest-error condition. validate-capabilities checks http_domains against allowlist, max_memory_mb and max_cpu_seconds against policy ceilings. Returns (values ok problems-list).

title: Phase 2: Ed25519 signature verification
description:
Verify Ed25519 signatures on skill WASM bundles via ironclad. Trust store at ~/.crichton/trusted-keys/. Only signed skills execute.

Notes: skills/signing.lisp: generate-signing-keypair (64-byte priv = seed+pub, 32-byte pub to trust store, key-id = hex first 8 bytes of pub), sign-skill-bytes, verify-skill-signature, load-trusted-keys (scans *.pub), verify-skill-bundle (tries all trusted keys against manifest :skill :signature). Fixed manifest path to read from nested :skill plist.

title: Fix: unused TIMEOUT parameter in remote-eval
description:
cli/remote.lisp remote-eval has a :timeout keyword parameter that is never used. Either wire it into the swank-client call or remove it.

Notes: Style-warning from SBCL compiler: The variable TIMEOUT is defined but never used.

title: Fix: unused M and H variables in next-daily-ut
description:
skills/builtins/scheduler.lisp:68 — decode-universal-time binds m and h but only uses day/month/year. Add (declare (ignore m h)) to suppress SBCL style-warning.

Notes: Style-warning from SBCL compiler: The variable M is defined but never used. The variable H is defined but never used.

title: Sanitize keyword interning from untrusted JSON
description:
credentials and sessions deserializers intern keywords from external JSON input. Malicious input could exhaust the keyword package. Should validate/whitelist allowed keywords or use strings internally.

Notes: Flagged during initial review. Affects credentials/backend-age-file.lisp and sessions/store.lisp.

title: Factor out duplicate TOML-to-plist helpers
description: skills/manifest.lisp and config/loader.lisp both have TOML-to-plist conversion functions. Factor into a shared utility module to eliminate duplication.

title: TOML string-vs-keyword normalization
description:
TOML config values arrive as strings but code defaults use keywords. Fixed in llm/registry.lisp by normalizing to keyword. Other consumers will need the same treatment as they come online.

Notes: Currently only llm/registry.lisp handles this. Watch for same issue in credential, session, and channel config consumers.

title: Weather skill bypasses egress control
description:
skills/builtins/weather.lisp makes direct HTTP calls via dexador without going through an allowlist check. Not critical (daemon-side built-in, not WASM) but inconsistent with the spec's egress control philosophy.

Notes: Built-in skills are part of the TCB so this is a consistency issue, not a vulnerability.

title: Session encryption toggle hazard
description:
Session files always use the .age extension regardless of whether the encrypt config setting is enabled. Could mislead about whether data is actually encrypted at rest.

Notes: Should use .jsonl extension when encryption is disabled, or refuse to disable encryption.

title: Implement libsecret credential backend
description:
OS keychain credential backend via libsecret (Linux desktop) is defined in the spec but only stubbed. Only the age-file backend is implemented.

Notes: cricht-026 specifies libsecret on Linux desktop, Security.framework on macOS. Both unimplemented — only age-file fallback works.

title: Local persistent data store
description:
Add a local persistent data store for Crichton. Immediate need: persist meter/usage statistics across daemon restarts. Broader use: local knowledge base, accumulated context, skill state. Candidates: SQLite via CFFI, or cl-store, or simple age-encrypted JSONL append logs. Must respect the security model — encrypted at rest, inaccessible to WASM skills except through mediated host functions.

Notes: Motivated by token-usage metering (currently in-memory only, lost on restart). Also useful for RSS seen-item tracking, scheduler task persistence, and future local knowledge/RAG.

title: Version information visibility
description:
Version info should be prominent and accessible in multiple contexts: Swank REPL banner on connect, daemon startup log message, system-info skill output, and the agent's own self-knowledge (system prompt or introspectable). Currently only available via 'crichton version' CLI command. Include git commit hash at build time if possible.

Notes: Troubleshooting is difficult without visible version. Consider embedding git rev via save-lisp-and-die build step.

title: Amp LLM coding/testing orchestration skill
description:
Built-in skill to interface with the Amp LLM agent. Allows Crichton to orchestrate coding tasks (write code, refactor, fix bugs) and testing tasks (run tests, interpret results, iterate on failures) by delegating to Amp. This makes Crichton a supervisor that can plan work and dispatch implementation to Amp.

Notes: Key design questions: communication protocol with Amp (CLI invocation, API, or MCP?), how to scope file access and repo context, how to feed back test results for iteration loops, cost tracking via the metering system.

title: GitHub skill
description:
Built-in skill for GitHub API interaction: repo management, issues, PRs, code search, actions/CI status, releases. Authenticate via personal access token stored in credential store. Dual interface (plist + report).

Notes: GitHub REST API v3 and/or GraphQL v4 via dexador. PAT stored as credential 'github-api-key'. Consider also supporting open-source forge alternatives: Forgejo, Gitea, GitLab — they share similar REST APIs so a generic forge protocol (CLOS) with per-forge adapters may be the right abstraction.

title: Phase 5: Discord bot channel adapter
description: Discord Gateway WebSocket v10 + REST API adapter. CLOS channel protocol, auto-reconnect, heartbeat, mention/DM filtering, per-conversation agent loop integration. Credential store for bot token, config-driven enable.
notes: Implements BEAD-022 spec decision. Gateway intents: GUILDS+GUILD_MESSAGES+DIRECT_MESSAGES+MESSAGE_CONTENT. No RESUME yet (reconnects with fresh IDENTIFY).

title: crichton status hangs when daemon not running
description:
When the daemon is not running, 'crichton status' hangs instead of returning quickly with an error.

The issue is in cli/remote.lisp - remote-eval-quiet catches errors but swank-client:slime-connect may have a long timeout or hang indefinitely when trying to connect to port 4005 when nothing is listening.

Fix options:
1. Add explicit timeout to swank-client connection
2. Use usocket with short timeout to test connectivity first
3. Add with-timeout wrapper around the connection attempt

Expected behavior: Should return immediately saying 'Crichton is not running' when daemon is down.

title: SHOWSTOPPER: Daemon crashes on startup with SWANK-CLIENT::CONNECTION-LOCK error
description:
The daemon immediately dies upon startup with an error:

  There is no applicable method for the generic function
  #<STANDARD-GENERIC-FUNCTION SWANK-CLIENT::CONNECTION-LOCK (1)>
  when called with arguments (NIL).

This appears to be in cli/remote.lisp where swank-client:slime-close is called with a NIL connection in the unwind-protect clause.

The issue occurs when:
1. remote-eval-quiet tries to connect to Swank
2. Connection fails (daemon not fully started yet)
3. unwind-protect calls (swank-client:slime-close connection) with connection=NIL
4. swank-client tries to access CONNECTION-LOCK on NIL, crashes

Fix: Check if connection is non-NIL before calling slime-close:
  (when connection
    (swank-client:slime-close connection))

This is blocking daemon startup and must be fixed immediately.
close_reason: Fixed by adding nil check before swank-client:slime-close

title: Revisit CLI architecture: standalone vs daemon-backed commands
description:
The need to add console logging suppression for standalone CLI commands (chat, weather, etc.) raises questions about the current architecture. Currently some commands talk to the daemon via Swank (stop, status, eval) while others run as completely separate instances (chat, weather, cred, doctor).

Consider consolidating to have more commands talk to the daemon rather than running standalone instances. This would:
- Avoid logging configuration duplication/issues
- Potentially share state and resources more efficiently
- Simplify the mental model

Trade-offs to evaluate:
- Startup latency for simple commands
- Dependency on daemon being running
- Complexity of remote invocation vs direct calls
- Session isolation vs state sharing

This is marked P3 as it's not urgent, but worth revisiting once the core functionality is stable.

title: SHOWSTOPPER: Memory faults in SBCL daemon process
description:
The daemon process is experiencing memory faults/corruption errors from SBCL. This is a critical stability issue that makes the daemon unusable.

Symptoms:
- Memory faults appearing in journalctl logs
- SBCL corruption warnings
- Process instability

Potential causes to investigate:
1. FFI/CFFI issues with wasmtime bindings
2. Thread safety issues (bordeaux-threads, websocket-driver)
3. Unsafe memory operations
4. Foreign function callbacks (host-log-callback in wasm/engine.lisp)
5. WebSocket library (websocket-driver) memory management

Investigation steps:
1. Check journalctl for specific error messages and stack traces
2. Review all FFI/CFFI code for safety
3. Check thread synchronization primitives
4. Review foreign callbacks for proper memory management
5. Consider running under valgrind or SBCL debug mode

This blocks production use of the daemon.

title: create a method to rebuild the cli manually
Unhandled promise rejection in setupCourseVisibilityHandlers
description: ModalUI.setupCourseVisibilityHandlers() calls StorageService.getVisibleCourses().then() without a .catch(). Storage failures become unhandled rejections.

title: Add input validation and sanitization
description: Implement comprehensive input validation for all user inputs to prevent injection attacks and data corruption. Key areas: StorageService (faculty name), parser.js (parseTimeToMinutes should throw/return null for invalid input), all data from chrome.storage before use. Validate format, length, and allowed characters. Sanitize before storage and before DOM insertion.

title: Debounce MutationObserver updatePageState calls
description: The MutationObserver in content.js can fire updatePageState() many times in rapid succession on dynamic pages. Add debounce/throttle (~250-500ms) to prevent redundant storage writes and DOM injection.

title: Test fixtures missing HtmlSanitizer dependency causing 7 test failures
description: CourseFilter.test.js and content.integration.test.js both fail because TimetableGenerator calls HtmlSanitizer._generateTableHeaderHTML but HtmlSanitizer.js is not loaded in the test setup. This causes 7 tests to fail with 'ReferenceError: HtmlSanitizer is not defined'. Need to add HtmlSanitizer to the test setup files or mock it.

title: Remove dead selection overlay in OfficeHoursManager
description: _createSelectionOverlay() creates and appends a DOM element that is never positioned, shown, or used. Selection feedback is done via cell background colors instead. Remove the dead overlay code.

title: Fix MutationObserver not reconnecting after tab hidden
description: content.js visibilitychange handler disconnects the MutationObserver when the tab is hidden but never re-observes when visible again. SPA detection breaks after tab switch. Either remove the visibility disconnect or add reconnect logic.

title: Remove unreachable findSlotIndex -1 check
description: findSlotIndex() never returns -1, but _calculateMeetingSlots checks for it. Either remove the dead check or update findSlotIndex to return -1 when time is before schedule start.

title: Deduplicate export payload in popup.js
description: downloadJSON() and copyToClipboard() build the same {term, termName, extractedAt, sections} object independently. Extract a shared buildExportData() helper.

title: Update project documentation with Beads workflow
description: Update project documentation to explain the Beads-based AI development workflow, including how to view tasks, claim work, and maintain context across sessions.

title: options.js should use StorageService instead of direct chrome.storage
description: options.js reads/writes chrome.storage.sync directly, bypassing StorageService validation and sanitization. Should use StorageService.getFacultyName() and setFacultyName() for consistency.

title: Popup URL validation too permissive (includes check)
description: popup.js _validateCamosunUrl() uses tab.url.includes('camosun.ca') which matches evilcamosun.ca or camosun.ca.evil.com. Should parse URL and validate hostname properly: u.hostname === 'camosun.ca' || u.hostname.endsWith('.camosun.ca').

title: ModalUI.printTimetable can create duplicate style tags
description: printTimetable() appends a <style> without removing an existing one first. Rapid successive print calls can create duplicates. Should remove existing style before appending.

title: Review and document current project state
description: Analyze the mycamosun-timetable codebase to understand current functionality, identify areas for improvement, and document the architecture. This will serve as a foundation for future AI-assisted development sessions.
close_reason: Completed comprehensive project state review. Created PROJECT_STATE_REVIEW.md with detailed analysis of architecture, testing, documentation, and recommendations for future work.

title: Implement visual timetable grid generator
description: Create a formatted visual timetable grid that displays course schedules in a calendar-like format. This should show time slots, days of the week, and course blocks with relevant information (course code, room, etc.).

title: Add try-catch blocks to all async functions
description: Add proper error handling with try-catch blocks to all async functions to prevent unhandled promise rejections. Key files: content.js (generateTimetable, updatePageState), popup.js (checkPageStatus, extractTimetable), CourseFilter.js (all async methods), OfficeHoursManager.js. Ensure errors are surfaced to users appropriately rather than failing silently.

title: Cache cell list in OfficeHoursManager drag selection
description: _updateCellSelection queries all td[data-day][data-slot] cells on every mousemove event. Cache the cell list once when entering selection mode and reuse it.

title: Content script message handlers don't catch parser errors
description: chrome.runtime.onMessage handler in content.js calls MyCamosunParser.parseTerm() and findAvailableTerms() without try/catch. If parser throws, sendResponse is never called and popup hangs.

title: OfficeHoursManager.enableOfficeHoursMode doesn't null-check button
description: enableOfficeHoursMode() calls _updateButtonForSelection(btn) without checking if btn is null. If the button element isn't found, it will throw.

title: StorageService getAll*() methods don't validate stored type
description: getAllOfficeHours(), getAllHiddenMeetings(), and getAllVisibleCourses() return result[key] || {} without checking the type. If storage is corrupted (string/array), callers crash on property assignment. Should use _isPlainObject() guard.

title: Extract shared button/hover-style helpers
description: content.js and ModalUI.js both implement button creation with manual JS hover handlers. Extract a shared helper or use CSS classes to reduce duplication.

title: Fix event listener leak in OfficeHoursManager
description: _createCleanupHandler creates new function references instead of using the ones added via addEventListener, so removeEventListener never works. Handlers accumulate each time office-hours mode is entered. Fix by passing actual handler references into the cleanup function.

title: Fix MutationObserver memory leak
description: The MutationObserver in content.js is never disconnected, causing a memory leak. Add cleanup handler to disconnect observer when page unloads or extension is disabled. Implement proper lifecycle management: store observer reference, add beforeunload handler to call observer.disconnect().

title: Add ICS calendar export functionality
description: Implement ICS (iCalendar) export functionality to allow users to import their course schedules into calendar applications like Google Calendar, Outlook, or Apple Calendar.
